LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS TOURCORP.COM INC. DBA THE SAN FRANCISCO ELECTRIC TOUR COMPANY, BRIAN HUBER AND THE SAN FRANCISCO ELECTRIC TOUR COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS HAVE MET THEIR INITIAL BURDEN TO SHOW THAT PLAINTIFFS EXECUTED A VALID RELEASE/WAIVER THAT CONTEMPLATES THE CIRCUMSTANCES OF MS. LAMB'S ACCIDENT. CONSEQUENTLY, PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT MET THEIR SHIFTING BURDEN. PLAINTIFFS' INABILITY TO VERIFY THE RELEASE/WAIVER PRODUCED BY DEFENDANTS DOES NOT CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. THE RELEASE/WAIVER ENUMERATES RIDING CONDITIONS THAT INCLUDE ROUGH OR MUDDY DIRT TRIALS, OBSTACLES AND HAZARDS INCLUDING TREES AND UNEVEN TERRAIN. PLAINTIFFS' EVIDENCE DOES NOT DEMONSTRATE THAT DEFENDANTS MADE ANY MISREPRESENTATIONS OR BREACHED ANY AGREEMENT TO REMAIN ON PAVED SURFACES. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.
MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-05-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-05-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Steven R. Disharoon, Esq. of Wood Smith Henning & Berman LLP, Tel. 925.222.3400, for defendants and moving party;
Lawrence Artenian, Esq. of Wagner & Jones LLP, Tel. 559.449.1800, for plaintiff and opposing party via Court Call;
Mark Venardi, Esq. of Venardi Zurada LLP, Tel. 925.937.3900, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS TOURCORP.COM INC. DBA THE SAN FRANCISCO ELECTRIC TOUR COMPANY, BRIAN HUBER AND THE SAN FRANCISCO ELECTRIC TOUR COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is Granted. Defendants have met their initial burden to show that Plaintiffs executed a valid Release/Waiver that contemplates the circumstances of Ms. Lamb's accident. Consequently, Plaintiffs have not met their shifting burden. Plaintiffs' inability to verify the Release/Waiver produced by Defendants does not create a triable issue of fact. The Release/Waiver enumerates riding conditions that include rough or muddy dirt trials, obstacles and hazards including trees and uneven terrain. Plaintiffs' evidence does not demonstrate that Defendants made any misrepresentations or breached any agreement to remain on paved surfaces. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.
